creator: fbsd@opal.com
text:
On Mon, 7 May 2012 01:00:46 UT, Edwin Groothuis <edwin@FreeBSD.ORG> wrote:
>
> Maintainer of games/daimonin-music,
> 
> Please note that PR ports/167661 has just been submitted.
> 
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
> 
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>     http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/167661
> 

It is correct that this port does not contain a checksum or version.

This is the music file for the games/daimonin port.  The music is
updated fairly regularly and the file name used for the current
version does not change.  The game works fine and does not break
if "old" music is still present.

In order to avoid endless updates to the port as well as endless
updates for users, the port was deliberately made to reflect the
origin site distribution without versioning.

As things stand, when the port is installed, the latest music is
what is installed.  If you deinstsall/reinstall, you're updated
to the latest.

There is no security issue with this, although admittedly, since
there's no checksum, you could conceivably be duped into installing
music other than that which was intended.

I have no plans to change this, but would be happy to hand over
maintainership to anyone who wants to take it over, but be advised
that adding a version number and checksum will not reflect the
origin, it will involve regular and repeated work for the port
maintainer and the committer, and users will be regularly, and
needlessly, updating.

	-jr
